company.name: Ferrite
company.one_liner: Content-addressed build cache that makes LLM-agent CI runs 10x cheaper by deduplicating agent-generated build steps across a fleet.

sections.snapshot:
**Ferrite** — Berlin-based solo technical founder Mara Voss; content-addressed remote build cache targeting agent-driven CI cost. **Ask:** $500k pre-seed; applying for Example Fund I **$100K fast check** (1–3% ownership target).

**Verified today:** Real open-source artifact with sustained ship cadence [C1], independent hackathon validation [C2], and launch engagement with self-identified production users [C3]. **Contested:** Deck-reported scale ($18k MRR, 3,100 active installs) is subject-sourced only [C4][C5] and **contradicted** on install count by public registry data [C6].

**Fit:** ai-infrastructure / devtools / applied-ai-b2b; pre-seed; EU geography; no disqualifiers triggered. **Decision posture:** contested — execution evidence supports engagement; commercial load-bearing claims require reconciliation before capital. [prov: C1,C6 / snapshot / sig-002,sig-004]
sections.hypotheses:
1. **Shipping velocity before pedigree** — A first-time founder with 11 months of high-commit cadence, weekly releases, and external contributors can earn a pre-track-record check if commercial claims reconcile modestly. [C1][C7] [prov: C1 / hypotheses / sig-002]

2. **Agent-CI cost is an incremental wedge** — Content-addressed dedup across agent fleets addresses a structurally growing spend line (repeated agent build steps) that incumbent caches may not optimize for fleet-level reuse; mechanism is coherent, benchmark unverified. [C8][C9] [prov: C8 / hypotheses / sig-001]

3. **Community pull converts to paid infra** — Hackathon win, GitHub traction, and launch-thread production users suggest early devtools PMF path if payer proof follows. [C2][C3][C10] [prov: C2 / hypotheses / sig-003]

4. **Commercial integrity gate** — Registry lifetime downloads (2,900) below deck active-install count (3,100) is a serious credibility flag, not rounding; revenue entirely deck-sourced. Thesis-aligned risk appetite does not waive reconciliation on load-bearing numbers. [C4][C5][C6] [prov: C6 / hypotheses / sig-004]
sections.swot:
**Strengths**
- Independently verified product execution: 1,400 commits / 11 months, 86% founder-authored, weekly releases 20 weeks, real CI/test coverage [C1].
- Third-party validation: Hack-Nation infrastructure track winner (~400 teams) [C2].
- Launch visibility and in-thread technical depth; three commenters self-identified as production users [C3].

**Weaknesses**
- Solo founder; cold-start (no prior fundraise, no prior company) [C7].
- All revenue and install-scale metrics deck-only [C4][C5]; registry contradicts install claim [C6].
- Prior fintech remote-cache résumé claim unverified [C11].

**Opportunities**
- Agent-driven CI loops inflate build minutes — Ferrite's fleet-level content-addressed dedup is a plausible budget-line pitch if benchmark holds [C8][C9].
- Early registry velocity (340 downloads/week) and GitHub community (412 stars, 61 forks, 9 external contributors) [C1][C10].

**Threats**
- Crowded remote-cache incumbents (Bazel/Gradle remote cache, GitHub Actions cache, Turborepo/Nx) may absorb agent workloads as features.
- Credibility gap on traction narrative may slow design-partner conversion and institutional follow-on.
sections.problem_product:
**Problem:** LLM-agent CI pipelines re-run similar build steps across fleets, inflating CI minutes and cloud spend. Deck positions Ferrite as cutting agent-driven CI cost ~10x via content-addressed deduplication across a fleet [C8][C9] — **claimed, unverified benchmark**.

**Product:** Open-source `ferrite-cache` package with content-addressed remote caching primitive; GitHub repo shows production-grade engineering (CI, test coverage, external contributors) [C1]. Positioning: devtools infra layer between agent orchestrators and existing CI runners — not crypto, not regulated medical, not services revenue.

**Mechanism assessment:** Content-addressed remote caching is a proven primitive; agent-fleet dedup is a coherent extension rather than category invention. Idea-vs-market verdict: survives-as-is pending reproducible benchmark on a design-partner workload.
sections.traction:
**Independently observable:** 412 GitHub stars, 61 forks, 9 external contributors; 1,400 commits over 11 months [C1]. Public registry: 340 downloads/week, 2,900 lifetime downloads [C10]. Launch post reached major aggregator front page ~6 hours; 214 points, 89 comments; founder answered 41 comments [C3].

**Subject-sourced only (deck):** 3,100 active installations across 240 organizations [C5]; $18k MRR growing 40% MoM for 3 months [C4]; two unnamed design partners (Series-B devtools co., AI lab) [C12]; 10x cost reduction internal benchmark [C9].

**Tension:** Lifetime registry downloads (2,900) fall **below** deck active-install count (3,100) — active installs cannot be grounded in public registry alone [C6]. No Stripe or analytics export on file. Traction narrative is ahead of independently corroborated commercial scale until reconciliation.
sections.team_history:
**Mara Voss** — solo founder, technical, Berlin. Claimed: 4 years ex-infra at mid-size fintech, built internal remote cache cutting CI spend 60%; first-time founder, no prior fundraise [C11].

**Verified execution signals:** 86% of 1,400 commits founder-authored; weekly release cadence; hackathon win; launch-thread technical depth [C1][C2][C3]. Founder Score (agent-derived): 71 [58, 80], medium confidence, cold_start true [C7].

**Gap:** Prior employment scope and outcomes are deck-only — no back-channel reference on file.
sections.technology_defensibility:
**Verified artifact:** Repository with 1,400 commits, weekly releases, CI config with real test coverage, 9 external contributors [C1]. Content-addressed caching implementation is inspectable in open source.

**Claimed differentiation:** Fleet-level dedup across agent-generated build steps yielding 10x cost reduction on agent CI workloads [C8][C9] — no independent benchmark archived.

**Defensibility read:** Execution moat is early (ship cadence, community). Technical moat depends on cache hit rates at scale on agent workloads — unproven. Incumbent caches could replicate fleet dedup as a feature; wedge durability requires demonstrated payer savings on agent-CI workloads, not GitHub stars alone.
sections.market_sizing:
**Structural tailwind (reconstructed):** Agent-driven CI loops are a growing cost center for AI labs and agent-heavy devtools companies — aligns with fund sectors (ai-infrastructure, devtools, applied-ai-b2b).

**Quantified market sizing:** Not disclosed in application or signals. No TAM/SAM deck extract on file.

**Adoption proxies (verified-online):** 340 downloads/week registry; 412 GitHub stars [C1][C10] — nascent, not scaled commercial footprint.
sections.competition:
**Incumbent layer:** Remote build caching already exists — Bazel/Gradle remote cache, GitHub Actions cache, Turborepo/Nx remote cache. Buyers may treat agent-CI dedup as incremental feature request to existing vendors rather than new budget line.

**Ferrite wedge:** Content-addressed dedup **across agent fleets** (not single-pipeline cache) — plausible but unproven as standalone procurement.

**Early signals vs. incumbents:** Community traction and hackathon validation suggest awareness; no head-to-head benchmark or named churn-from-incumbent evidence on file.

gaps.note: Not disclosed in application or signals.
No Stripe or payment-processor export on file; $18k MRR and 40% MoM are deck-only (sig-001).
No product analytics export; deck count (3,100) unreconciled with registry lifetime downloads (2,900) per sig-004.
Design partner names withheld; no reference calls completed.
Internal benchmark claimed in deck; no reproducible before/after artifact on file.
Fintech remote-cache background is founder-claimed only; no back-channel reference on file.
No quantified market data in deck extract or signals.

diligence_log.instrument: Archived sig-002: 412 stars, 1,400 commits, weekly releases, CI/test coverage — independently verified product artifact.
Archived sig-003: Hack-Nation May 2026 infrastructure track winner, ~400 teams.
Archived sig-005: aggregator front page ~6h, 214 points, three self-identified production users in comments.
Archived sig-004: 340 downloads/week, 2,900 lifetime — contradicts deck active-install count (sig-001).
Request Stripe or payment-processor trailing-3-month revenue export with month-over-month cohort lines.

diligence_log.item: Active installation count reconciliation (3,100 vs registry 2,900 lifetime)
diligence_log.instrument: Request product analytics export with install vs active-weekly-user breakdown, including self-hosted and off-registry deployments; cross-walk to sig-004 registry totals.
Reproducible benchmark on one named design-partner agent-CI workload with cache hit rate and $/run before/after.
Two reference calls (Series-B devtools co., AI lab) with permission — confirm production agent-CI deployment and spend saved.
One back-channel reference call to prior employer infra lead confirming scope and CI spend outcomes.

conditions: Run pre-decision diligence bundle before wire: (1) Stripe trailing-3-month export with MoM cohorts, (2) product analytics active-install cross-walk including off-registry/self-hosted, (3) one design-partner reference call attesting production agent-CI use with cache-hit or cost improvement.
Gate A — both bull and bear poles authorize $100K write: ≥~$12k MRR reconciled, ≥2 named paying accounts, auditable install definition reconciled to registry plus documented off-registry gap.

Gate B — investor explicit thesis posture required: below Gate A but above zero (any modest verified revenue OR one confirmed production deployment AND benchmark holds at early scale) — choose execution-option vs commercial-integrity gate per fund risk appetite.
Gate C — pass: effectively zero verified revenue AND no design-partner attestation of deployed production use.
Treat deck installation scale and revenue as uncorroborated and non-load-bearing until reconciliation; registry–deck install gap is a serious credibility flag, not a rounding error.
decision.rationale: Agent-derived recommendation to human gate: Ferrite clears fund sector/stage/geography fit and disqualifier screen. Independently verified execution (sig-002, sig-003, sig-005) supports the fund's shipping-velocity thesis for pre-track-record founders. Commercial load-bearing claims (sig-001) remain subject-sourced and contradicted on install scale (sig-004 vs sig-001). Forum adjudication converged on reconciliation-before-capital for revenue/install figures while accepting technical artifact credibility. Do not wire $100K on deck claims alone; run the bundled diligence instrument, apply bifurcated Gate A/B/C, then human decides within 24h. Residual fork: fund's stated appetite for cold-start technical founders vs. commercial-integrity gate on unreconciled traction — only the human investor resolves after bundle results.
